issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: ET.parse is used without disabling external entity processing or entity expansion. The code does not set parser = ET.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False) or use defusedxml. This allows XXE and XML bomb attacks.

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: ET.parse is used without disabling external entity processing or entity expansion. The code does not set parser = ET.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False) or use defusedxml. This allows XXE and XML bomb attacks.

issues.description: This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
issues.judge_reason: The filename is extracted from the raw POST body using a fragile string split, then passed through sanitize_filename which only blocks absolute paths, '..', and null bytes. This is insufficient to prevent path traversal. The file is opened for writing, allowing an attacker to write outside uploads/.

issues.code: if not os.path.exists(safe_data_path) or not os.path.exists(safe_config_path):
issues.description: This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
issues.judge_reason: The report_name parameter is user-controlled and passed through sanitize_filename, which is insufficient. The resulting safe_data_path and safe_config_path are used in os.path.exists, allowing an attacker to probe for files outside uploads/.

duplicate_findings.code:
2 import sqlite3
3 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
4 from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
duplicate_findings.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: xml.etree.ElementTree import is flagged because the code does not use defusedxml or disable entity expansion. This is the same root cause as B314.

duplicate_findings.description: XML parsing depends on a [user-provided value](1) without guarding against uncontrolled entity expansion.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: ET.parse is used without guarding against uncontrolled entity expansion (billion laughs attack). The code does not set entity_limits or use a safe parser.

duplicate_findings.description: This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Same root cause as line 33: config_filename is also user-controlled and insufficiently sanitized.

duplicate_findings.description: This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: safe_config_path is derived from user input (report_name) with insufficient sanitization. ET.parse reads the file, enabling an attacker to read arbitrary XML files on the filesystem.

duplicate_findings.judge_reason: safe_data_path is derived from user input (report_name) with insufficient sanitization. The file is opened for reading, allowing an attacker to read arbitrary files.
